title: senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. rodriguezii
intro: Senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. rodriguezii, also known as Rodriguez's Jacob's Ladder or Canary Islands Ragwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is a perennial plant native to the Canary Islands, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Traditionally, Senecio leucanthemifolius subsp. rodriguezii is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity.
